user.title: Freematics ONE+ – Connection Type
user.description: Choose how your Freematics ONE+ device will connect to the internet to send data to Home Assistant.

wifi.title: Freematics ONE+ – WiFi Credentials
wifi.description: Enter the WiFi network credentials that the Freematics ONE+ should connect to.

data_description.wifi_ssid: → **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) · **Send Config** button
data_description.wifi_password: → **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) · **Send Config** button

cellular.title: Freematics ONE+ – Cellular / SIM
cellular.description: Enter the APN for your cellular provider. Leave blank to use the default APN. The SIM PIN is only required if your SIM card has a PIN lock.

data_description.cell_apn: → **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) · **Send Config** button

webhook.title: Freematics ONE+ – Webhook / Firmware Settings
webhook.description:
A unique webhook has been generated for your device.

Copy the settings below into your firmware configuration:

**SERVER_HOST**: `{ha_host}`
**SERVER_PORT**: `443`
**HA_WEBHOOK_ID**: `{webhook_id}`

Full webhook URL (for reference):
`{webhook_url}`

You can edit the Webhook ID below if you want to use a custom value.

device.title: Freematics ONE+ – Device Model
device.description:
Select your Freematics ONE+ hardware model. This helps tailor the recommended firmware settings.

- **Model A**: WiFi + Bluetooth, no cellular module
- **Model B**: WiFi + Bluetooth + 4G LTE cellular
- **Model H**: WiFi only (no Bluetooth, no cellular)

Detailed hardware specifications at [freematics.com](https://freematics.com).

advanced.title: Freematics ONE+ – Operating Mode & OTA
advanced.description:
Choose how the device should operate after flashing. This controls which firmware features are enabled.

**Telelogger** *(recommended for Home Assistant users)*: Pushes telemetry to the HA webhook. Keep HTTPD and BLE *disabled* to free ~104 KB of heap for the TLS connection — this prevents the `MBEDTLS_ERR_SSL_ALLOC_FAILED (-32512)` error.

**Datalogger**: Enables the built-in HTTP server (HTTPD) on port 80, exposing `/api/live`, `/api/info`, and related REST endpoints for local network access. Useful without Home Assistant or alongside a local dashboard.

**BLE** is independent of both modes. Enable it only if you use the Freematics Controller App over Bluetooth.

**Cellular debug logging**: Enable to print verbose cellular diagnostics (TX-Preview, hex-dump, AT+CCHSTATUS? and incoming-data notifications) to the serial console. Use only for troubleshooting; disable in normal operation.

**LED settings**: The red/power LED lights up while the device is on or in standby. The white/network LED flashes briefly during each data transmission over WiFi or cellular. Both can be disabled independently to reduce light in the vehicle cabin.

**Beep**: The buzzer emits a short beep each time a WiFi or cellular connection is established. Disable to suppress in-cabin noise.

**Intervals**: leave at `0` to keep firmware defaults (~1000 ms / 120 s).

data.led_red_en: Enable red/power LED (lights up when device is on or in standby)
data.led_white_en: Enable white/network LED (flashes during WiFi/cellular data transmission)
data.beep_en: Enable connection beep (short buzzer sound on WiFi/cellular connect)

data_description.led_red_en: → **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) · **Send Config** button
data_description.led_white_en: → **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) · **Send Config** button
data_description.beep_en: → **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) · **Send Config** button
data_description.ota_mode:
**Disabled** – no OTA. **Variant 1 (Pull-OTA)** – device automatically downloads the latest bundled firmware on every interval. **Variant 2 (Cloud OTA)** – only downloads after you press **Publish Firmware for Cloud OTA**. Both variants use a secret token written to device NVS during flashing.

→ **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) — *initial provisioning requires serial flash*
data_description.ota_check_interval_s:
How often the device checks for updates. Set to 0 to disable polling. The OTA token and interval are written to NVS during flashing — re-flash after changing these settings.

→ **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) · **Send Config** button

flash.title: Freematics ONE+ – Flash Method
flash.description:
Choose how to flash the firmware onto the device.

**Browser Flasher** *(recommended)*: Uses the Web Serial API (Chrome/Edge 89+) and runs directly in your browser. The device must be connected via USB to the computer where your browser is running — not to the HA server. Open the [Browser Flasher]({flasher_url}) from the Freematics sidebar panel. All settings (WiFi, server, OTA token) are embedded automatically.

**Serial USB (HA server)**: Runs `esptool` on the Home Assistant server. Only works if the Freematics ONE+ is connected via USB **directly to the HA server**. All settings including OTA token and interval are written to NVS automatically.

After completing setup, use the **Flash Firmware** buttons in the integration to start the flash process.

For detailed instructions see the [documentation](https://github.com/northpower25/Freematics/blob/master/docs/README.md#firmware-flashing).

data_description.flash_method: → **HA-only** — controls how HA flashes the device; not written to device NVS
data_description.device_ip: → **HA-only** — used by HA for WiFi OTA upload and Send Config; not written to device NVS
data_description.device_port: → **HA-only** — used by HA for WiFi OTA upload and Send Config; not written to device NVS
data_description.serial_port: → **HA-only** — used by HA for serial flash; not written to device NVS

abort.already_configured: This webhook ID is already configured.

init.title: Freematics ONE+ – Update Settings
init.description:
Update the device settings. Changes are applied on the next flash or when you press **Send Config to Device**.

⚠️ After changing OTA settings, re-provision the device (re-flash NVS via the Browser Flasher or Serial Flash button) so the new token and interval are written to the device.

data.led_red_en: Enable red/power LED (lights up when device is on or in standby)
data.led_white_en: Enable white/network LED (flashes during WiFi/cellular data transmission)
data.beep_en: Enable connection beep (short buzzer sound on WiFi/cellular connect)

data_description.wifi_ssid: → **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) · **Send Config** button
data_description.wifi_password: → **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) · **Send Config** button
data_description.cell_apn: → **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) · **Send Config** button
data_description.led_red_en: → **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) · **Send Config** button
data_description.led_white_en: → **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) · **Send Config** button
data_description.beep_en: → **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) · **Send Config** button
data_description.ota_mode:
**Disabled** – no automatic updates. **Variant 1 (Pull-OTA)** – device polls HA every interval and downloads the latest bundled firmware. **Variant 2 (Cloud OTA)** – device polls HA but only downloads after you press the **Publish Firmware for Cloud OTA** button on the device page.

→ **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) — *initial provisioning requires serial flash*
data_description.ota_check_interval_s:
How often (seconds) the device checks for a new firmware version. Set to 0 to disable polling even when a variant is selected. Requires re-provisioning the device (re-flash) after changes.

→ **Applied via:** Serial flash · OTA (WiFi & Cellular) · **Send Config** button
data_description.device_ip: → **HA-only** — used by HA for WiFi OTA upload and Send Config; not written to device NVS
data_description.device_port: → **HA-only** — used by HA for WiFi OTA upload and Send Config; not written to device NVS
data_description.flash_method: → **HA-only** — controls how HA flashes the device; not written to device NVS
data_description.serial_port: → **HA-only** — used by HA for serial flash; not written to device NVS
